texto: It feels like I’m trapped in quicksand. The more I struggle, the more I sink. So I stop struggling. I stop trying to free myself; because the more I struggle the scarier it becomes. Then—and only then did panic yield long enough for a numbness to spread and stick to me like a second skin.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Jannet Casas: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a autoestima. O trecho “It feels like I’m trapped in quicksand. The more I struggle, the more I sink. So I stop strugglin…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que autoestima, anst, coming-of-age pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de coming-of-age e coming-of-age-novel.

frase_original: It feels like I’m trapped in quicksand. The more I struggle, the more I sink. So I stop struggling. I stop trying to free myself; because the more I struggle the scarier it becomes. Then—and only then did panic yield long enough for a numbness to spread and stick to me like a second skin.
